action.reasoning: Observation: The best submitted checkpoint is still the working CLIP-B stable feature-head at macro_f1=0.1749; the attempted post-hoc calibration did not improve and may have been overfit or ignored. Analysis: With few iterations left, the safest improvement is to keep the proven predict.py feature_head path and create a new checkpoint using the same CLIP-B cached features but training on both train and public OOD-val labels with strong class-balanced sampling/loss, saving in the same feature_head format. This uses additional labeled data and may improve test OOD class coverage while preserving the old best if it fails. Decision: Write a separate train_features_combo.py that reuses cached features, trains multiple balanced linear heads on train+val with conservative regularization/EMA, embeds the required checkpoint metadata, then submit the resulting checkpoint for evaluation.
